meta.title: College Tour 2026
meta.travelers: Dietrich, Theo & Simona — joined by Silke (Fri–Mon)
meta.disclaimer: Times are planning targets; align with booked slots and confirm live schedules & rates.

summary.legs: Leg 1: Rochester → New York (Jul 18–25, car) • Leg 2: Philadelphia → Washington DC (Jul 25–31, car)
summary.flights: Sat Jul 18 SAN→IAD; onward upstate-NY flights cancelled by storms — overnight Reston (Dulles). Sun Jul 19 UA4335 IAD→Buffalo (BUF) + Ithaca Limousine BUF→ROC (rebooked). Dietrich home Jul 25 EWR 4:59 PM→SAN (JX3T42) · Simona Jul 25 AA 2955 → PHL 2:40 PM
summary.cars: Leg 1: Hertz Camry — pick up ROC Sun Jul 19 ~4:30 PM (conf. L6042669403) → EWR Jul 25. Leg 2: Avis — PHL 3:30 PM Jul 25 → DCA 8:30 AM Jul 31 (conf. 20655662US6)
summary.keyEvents: World Cup Final Sun Jul 19 · The Odyssey IMAX 70mm Wed Jul 22 9:45 PM, Providence · Red Sox Tue Jul 21 7:10 PM · Mets Fri Jul 24 7:10 PM · Phillies Sun Jul 26

entries.text: SAN → Washington Dulles (IAD).
Onward flights to upstate NY cancelled by Northeast thunderstorms; overnight near Dulles (Reston). The Courtyard Rochester (C1) was cancelled — no Rochester overnight now.

timeline.label: Sun Jul 19
timeline.title: Rebooked BUF → U of R walk + World Cup → Ithaca

entries.text: Breakfast / check out at Reston (Dulles).
UA4335 IAD → Buffalo (BUF).
Ithaca Limousine pickup at BUF (they rebooked us) → drive to Rochester (~1 hr 15).
Arrive ROC airport; pick up the Hertz Camry (conf. L6042669403). Allow ~15–20 min at the counter.
Drive to the U of R River Campus (~10 min — airport is adjacent). Park Library / Susan B. Anthony lot (free weekends).
University of Rochester — self-guided campus walk (~75 min). Briefing + map in section D.
Short drive to The Distillery (Mt. Hope, ~5 min).
Early dinner with Elise at The Distillery. World Cup Final (kickoff ~3 PM ET) caught on the road en route.
Depart for Ithaca (~1 hr 30).
Check into Ithaca Marriott Downtown; dinner on the Commons if hungry. Overnight Ithaca.

timeline.note: The Sun 8:00 AM Rochester Odyssey showing was missed in the reroute and refunded — rebooked to Providence, Wed Jul 22.

entries.text: Wake.
Workout, 8:00–9:30 (hotel fitness center or a run — the Cascadilla Gorge trail is right by the Commons).
Shower, pack, check out — bags in the car.
Brunch at Milkstand (215 N Meadow St) — hearty local diner, a Cornell-visit favorite.
Drive to campus (~10 min); park Forest Home Garage (reserved ePermit) — walk to the Tang Welcome Center.
On campus, 30 min before the tour. Briefing + walking map in section D2.
Cornell General Campus Tour, 1:00–2:15 PM (conf. CU5856UY).
Depart Ithaca for Boston (~5 hr 15 driving).
Dinner on the road — Wegmans Natick (1245 Worcester St), right off the Mass Pike: sit-down Pub upstairs + a big prepared-foods hall with seating. ~30 min out from Cambridge.
Arrive Cambridge; check into Residence Inn Boston Cambridge (Kendall). Overnight (2 nts).

timeline.label: Tue Jul 21
timeline.title: Harvard, Tufts, MIT bookstore + Fenway
timeline.alert: Rain — Tue: showers likely with possible PM thunderstorms (Tufts' 2 PM slot is the wettest window). Pack the rain shells; hands-free beats umbrellas on a moving tour. The MIT-bookstore stop doubles as a dry-afternoon backup.

entries.text: Walk/T to Harvard (~10 min); check in at Agassiz House, Radcliffe Yard. Mobile check-in once within 1.2 mi.
Harvard information session (~45 min) — no tour.
Self-guided Harvard Yard walk (~60–75 min) — briefing + map in section D3.
Lunch in Harvard Square.
Drive to Tufts (Medford, ~20 min); GPS to the Boston Ave garage entrance, 419 Boston Ave.
Tufts campus tour — check in Eaton Hall, 5 The Green. Arrive ~20 min early. Briefing + map in D4.
MIT bookstores at Kendall (steps from the hotel): MIT Press Bookstore, 314 Main St (science / philosophy — Theo's shelf) + MIT COOP, 80 Broadway. Both close 6 PM.
Head to Fenway (~20 min).
Red Sox vs. Orioles @ Fenway. Overnight Cambridge.

timeline.label: Wed Jul 22
timeline.title: MIT, BU (walk-by), Boston College → Providence + The Odyssey
timeline.alert: Parking, locked in (no spot-hunting): MIT — walk from the hotel, car stays put. BU — Agganis Arena Garage, 925 Comm Ave (Warren Towers closed). BC — Commonwealth Ave Garage via GPS "2004 Campanella Way," floors 3–6, then rooftop → Devlin Hall. Heads-up: the ~12:37 AM movie finish sits right before Brown check-in at 9:30 AM Thu — a short night before the trip's tightest day. BU is the flex stop if you're running behind after MIT.

entries.text: Breakfast; check out and load the car (you leave Cambridge tonight — bags stay in the car all day).
Leave the car in the hotel garage and walk to the MIT Welcome Center, 292 Main St (~6 min). No need to move the car for MIT.
MIT info session + tour (~90 min). Briefing + walking map in D5.
Back to the hotel garage; depart for BU (~15 min over the BU Bridge).
Park: Agganis Arena Garage, 925 Comm Ave (public, ~$2/hr, easy in/out). NOTE: the central Warren Towers garage is closed all summer.
BU self-guided walk-by (quick) + grab lunch. Briefing + map in D6.
Sneakers en route to BC — The Shops at Chestnut Hill, 199 Boylston St (Rte 9): Bloomingdale's + more, ~5 min from BC.
Park: BC Commonwealth Ave Garage — GPS "2004 Campanella Way." Take a ticket; park floors 3–6; elevator to the 7th-floor rooftop → ~2-min walk across O'Neill Plaza to Devlin Hall.
At Devlin Hall, checked in ~30 min early (bring the parking ticket — admission may validate it).
Boston College info session + tour (~90 min). Briefing + map in D7.
Depart BC → Providence (~50 min via I-95 S) — straight through, no detour.
Check into Aloft Providence Downtown (191 Dorrance St, Providence, RI 02903); drop bags.
Gym session at the Aloft fitness center.
Dinner at The Capital Grille, 10 Memorial Blvd, Providence — complimentary valet; reserve ahead. ~3-min walk to Apple Cinemas.
The Odyssey — IMAX 70mm, Apple Cinemas Providence Place. Ends ~12:37 AM.

timeline.label: Thu Jul 23
timeline.title: Brown → Yale (+ Jonathan Edwards College)
timeline.alert: Parking, locked in: Brown — Power Street Garage, 111 Power St (~10-min uphill walk to the Welcome Center). Yale — Grove Street Garage, 65 Grove St (~3-min walk to SSS Hall). No spot-hunting on College Hill or in downtown New Haven. Tight leg: Brown's tour ends ~11:30 and the Providence→New Haven drive runs ~1 hr 50 — leaving by ~11:45 lands you ~1:35, right for the 2 PM Yale check-in. If Brown runs long, head straight to Yale and see the campus first; the tour start is the fixed point.

entries.text: Breakfast; check out of the Aloft and load the car — you leave Providence straight after Brown.
Depart the Aloft; drive up College Hill (~10 min, 1.3 mi).
Park at the Power Street Garage — GPS: 111 Power Street (~$18/day flat).
Walk ~10–12 min uphill to Brown's Admission Welcome Center, 21 Prospect St — check in ~15 min early. Briefing + map in D8.
Brown info session + student panel + tour (~90 min).
Back to the garage; grab-and-go lunch; depart for New Haven.
Depart Providence → New Haven (~1 hr 50 via I-95 S). Leave promptly — the tight leg for the 2 PM tour.
Dietrich's work Zoom (12–1 PM ET / 9–10 AM PT) — hands-free, camera off, from the car while Theo co-pilots.
New Haven — park at the Grove Street Garage, 65 Grove St; walk ~3 min to SSS Hall, 1 Prospect St. Briefing + map in D9.
Yale campus tour (~70 min) — summer tours meet at SSS Hall.
Jonathan Edwards College, 68 High St (~10-min walk) — Yale's oldest residential college.
Check into Courtyard New Haven at Yale (30 Whalley Ave).
Dinner at BAR — 254 Crown St (~15-min walk): New Haven apizza, packed with Yale students, across from historic Louis' Lunch. Walk in early to beat the crowd. Overnight New Haven.

timeline.label: Fri Jul 24
timeline.title: Ferry (7:30) → pick up Silke → Stony Brook → Brooklyn → Mets

entries.text: Grab-and-go breakfast, check out.
Drive to Bridgeport ferry terminal, 1 Ferry Access Rd (~30–35 min).
Car ferry Bridgeport → Port Jefferson (75 min) — conf. 20260717-32111128.
Arrive Port Jefferson, 102 W Broadway; coffee/bagels in the village.
Pick up Silke at Port Jefferson Station (LIRR) — she joins through Monday.
Stony Brook — self-guided walk (~90 min): map at Admissions, Academic Mall, library, Wang Center, science quad.
Lunch (Port Jefferson / Stony Brook).
Drive to Park Slope, Brooklyn (~1 hr 45 via LIE/BQE).
Arrive Silke's in Park Slope; park the car, start laundry, settle in.
Subway together to Citi Field (~45–60 min).
Mets vs. Dodgers @ Citi Field (Dietrich, Theo, Silke + Sam). Subway back to Park Slope; overnight.
Depart Park Slope; coffee/bagels en route.
Drive to Fordham Rose Hill, 2691 Southern Blvd, Bronx (~40 min); self-guided walk (~45–60 min).
Drive Fordham → Newark (EWR), ~45–60 min.
At EWR — the group splits:
Return the Hertz car; United terminal. UA 210, EWR 4:59 PM → SAN.
AirTrain → Newark Airport rail station → Amtrak NE Regional 147, EWR 1:12 PM → Philadelphia 30th St 2:19 PM (conf. 97BF45, seats 3A/B).
AA 2955 lands PHL ~2:40 PM; pick up the Avis rental at PHL, 3:30 PM (conf. 20655662US6), drive to meet the others.
Silke, Theo & Simona converge in Philadelphia; check into the Canopy. Leg 2 begins.
Check into Canopy by Hilton Philadelphia Center City, 1180 Ludlow St; dinner near Reading Terminal Market. Overnight Philly (night 1).
Independence Hall, Liberty Bell, Old City, Reading Terminal Market, Art Museum steps.
Early dinner at Pizzeria Beddia, 1313 N Lee St, Fishtown — party of 5 (Simona, Theo, Silke + 2 Philly friends). Reserve ahead.
Uber to Citizens Bank Park (~15–20 min).
Phillies vs. Yankees @ Citizens Bank Park — Silke joins. Overnight Philly (night 2).
Silke takes the train back to NYC. Simona + Theo check out of the Canopy by noon (bags in car).
Penn info session; 10:15 AM tour — Cohen Hall, University City.
Villanova Nova Nation presentation + tour (~30 min drive; free I-1 Garage).
Drive to Princeton (~1 hr 15); stay with Raphaela (2 nts). Overnight Princeton.
Princeton info session + tour (~2 hrs), 36 University Place. Explore Nassau St. Overnight Princeton.
Drive Princeton → Baltimore (~2 hr 45); lunch near Homewood.
Johns Hopkins session + tour, 2:15–4:15 PM, Mason Hall (South Garage).
Drive Baltimore → Potomac, MD (~1 hr); stay with Nicole. Overnight Potomac.
Drive Potomac → Grosvenor–Strathmore park-and-ride (~15 min); Red Line into the city.
GWU Foggy Bottom tour (~1 hr), 800 21st St NW (transfer to Foggy Bottom/GWU station).
Georgetown info session + tour (~2 hrs) — bus/rideshare from Foggy Bottom.
Metro back to Grosvenor; drive to Potomac. Overnight Potomac (Nicole).
Drive Potomac → Reagan National (DCA); drop the Avis car, 2600 Richmond Hwy (conf. 20655662US6).
Simona & Theo fly home from DCA (flight TBD). Trip ends.

open.detail: Confirm Simona & Theo home from DCA, Fri Jul 31 (after the 8:30 AM car drop).
Reserve dinner for 8:00 PM, party of 2. 10 Memorial Blvd, Providence Place — complimentary valet, ~3-min walk to Apple Cinemas.

cancelled: Courtyard Rochester (C1) — storm reroute, never used

campusVisits.checkin: River Campus — start at Wilson Commons. Park at Library / Susan B. Anthony lot (free weekends).
campusVisits.parking: Free weekend parking at the Library / Susan B. Anthony lots.
campusVisits.notes: Follow the 8-stop self-guided loop below. Campus profile includes the full walking tour with map.

campusVisits.checkin: Martin Y. Tang Welcome Center at Noyes Lodge; ends Ho Plaza. Arrive 30 min early.
campusVisits.parking: RESERVED — Forest Home Garage (ParkMobile ePermit, valid all day Jul 20; covered). Bring printed ePermit for dash.
campusVisits.notes: Watch the recorded info session in advance. Guest: Dietrich Haubenberger.

campusVisits.type: Information Session ONLY — no guided tour
campusVisits.checkin: Elizabeth Cary Agassiz House, Radcliffe Yard, 77 Brattle St — enter via Brattle / Mason / Garden St gates.
campusVisits.parking: Street parking not advised (2-hr max) — use area garages. No luggage storage.
campusVisits.notes: Mobile check-in once within 1.2 mi of campus (Chrome + location services on). Allow +30–60 min travel.

campusVisits.checkin: Eaton Hall, 5 The Green (Dowling garage elevator to the 7th floor). Arrive ≥20 min early.
campusVisits.parking: FREE for registered visitors — Dowling Hall garage, 419 Boston Ave. GPS to Boston Ave entrance (summer construction closes North Hill Rd). Plates scanned; register vehicle online day-of. If full → Cousens Lot, 161 College Ave.
campusVisits.notes: Rideshare drop: 180 Packard Ave (Academic Quad). One last reminder sent 4 hrs prior.

campusVisits.checkin: MIT Welcome Center, 292 Main St (Kendall/MIT T). Arrive 15 min early.
Park near Marsh Plaza / Comm Ave.
campusVisits.notes: Walk: Marsh Plaza → BU Beach → Comm Ave → 'Jenga' data-sciences tower → Esplanade. Lunch on Comm Ave.

campusVisits.type: Eagle Eye Visit (Theo + 1). Middle Campus only (no residence halls / athletics shown). No recording.
campusVisits.checkin: Devlin Hall, 140 Commonwealth Ave. Arrive 20–30 min early; bring your parking ticket.
campusVisits.parking: Drive to 2004 Campanella Way → Commonwealth Ave Garage, floors 3–6; elevator/stairs to the 7th floor, follow signs to Devlin. If full → Beacon Street Garage. Contact 617-552-3100.

campusVisits.type: Info session + student panel + tour (~90 min). Party of 2 confirmed.
campusVisits.checkin: Admission Welcome Center, 21 Prospect St. Arrive ~15 min early (9:15 AM). Walk ~8 min from Power Street Garage.
campusVisits.parking: Power Street Garage, 111 Power St (~$18/day flat rate) — most convenient for Brown. Walk ~8 min up to Admission Welcome Center.
campusVisits.notes: Tours run in most weather. Briefing + map in D8.

campusVisits.checkin: SSS (Sheffield-Sterling-Strathcona) Hall, 1 Prospect St — Grove St side entrance. Arrive 5 min early.
campusVisits.parking: Grove Street Garage, 65 Grove St — ~3-min walk to SSS Hall. ParkMobile or pay at the machine.
campusVisits.notes: Tours depart rain or shine. Briefing + map in D9.

campusVisits.checkin: 68 High St, ~10 min from SSS Hall.
campusVisits.notes: One of Yale's oldest residential colleges, Gothic courtyard. Colleges are gated; courtyard/exterior is the public view. Inside needs a Yale affiliate to swipe in.

campusVisits.checkin: Stop at Undergraduate Admissions (Administration Building) for a self-guided map.
campusVisits.parking: Visitor parking by the Administration Building.
campusVisits.notes: Walk: Academic Mall → Frank Melville Library → Charles B. Wang Center → Student Activities Center → Life Sciences / science quad.

campusVisits.checkin: 2691 Southern Blvd, Bronx NY 10458. Ask campus security for a self-guided booklet.
campusVisits.notes: Any time 9 AM–5 PM. ~40 min from Park Slope. University quiet on weekends (limited access).

campusVisits.type: 9:30 AM Info Session + 10:15 AM Tour (2 attendees)
campusVisits.checkin: Undergraduate Admissions Visitor Center, Claudia Cohen Hall, Ground Floor (University City).
Mullen Center for the Performing Arts, Lobby — 800 E Lancaster Ave. Arrive 15 min early.
campusVisits.parking: Free in the I-1 Garage (~30 min drive from Center City).
campusVisits.notes: Final visit details emailed ~48 hrs prior.

campusVisits.type: Info session + student-led tour (~2 hrs; party of 2)
campusVisits.checkin: Admission Information Center, 36 University Place. Arrive 30 min early.
campusVisits.parking: Stadium Drive Garage (register vehicle day-of) + free Tiger Transit shuttle.

campusVisits.checkin: Lobby, 1st floor of Mason Hall — 3101 Wyman Park Dr, Baltimore.
campusVisits.parking: South Garage beneath Mason Hall (hourly; no validation).

campusVisits.type: In-Person Foggy Bottom Campus Tour (~1 hr)
campusVisits.checkin: Admissions Welcome Center, University Student Center, 800 21st St NW. Arrive 10 min early.
campusVisits.parking: From Grosvenor park-and-ride: Red Line → transfer at Metro Center to Foggy Bottom/GWU.

campusVisits.checkin: Office of Undergraduate Admissions, White-Gravenor Hall, Room 103 (first stone building past the gates).
campusVisits.parking: Main gates 3700 O St NW. No Metro — bus/rideshare from Foggy Bottom (~15 min).

eventsAndTransport.title: The Odyssey (IMAX 70mm)
eventsAndTransport.where: Apple Cinemas Providence Place, 10 Providence Place, Providence RI — Screen 17, seats B16 & B15 (2 adult).
eventsAndTransport.detail: Runtime 2 hr 52 min — starts 9:45 PM, ends ~12:37 AM. ~10-min walk / short drive from the Aloft (191 Dorrance St). $53 paid. Replaces the missed/refunded Sun 8 AM Rochester showing.
eventsAndTransport.alert: Late finish sits before Brown check-in 9:30 AM Thu — plan a short night.

eventsAndTransport.where: Citi Field, Section 407, Row 3 — 4 tickets: Dietrich + Theo, seats 5–6; Silke + Sam, seats 8–9.

eventsAndTransport.where: 1 Ferry Access Rd, Bridgeport CT → 102 W Broadway, Port Jefferson NY.
eventsAndTransport.detail: Car + driver + 1 adult passenger (75-min crossing). Arrive ~30 min early.

eventsAndTransport.title: Amtrak — Silke + Theo

eventsAndTransport.detail: AirTrain from EWR to the Newark Liberty airport rail station for the train.

eventsAndTransport.title: World Cup Final
eventsAndTransport.where: The Distillery, 1142 Mt Hope Ave (by College Town, ~5 min from U of R). (585) 271-4105.
eventsAndTransport.detail: Caught on the road during the BUF→ROC drive (radio/stream). Meet Elise for an early dinner at The Distillery ~6:30 PM after the U of R walk. Fine for under-21 with a parent.

eventsAndTransport.title: Leg 2 Rental Car — Avis

eventsAndTransport.detail: Pick-up Sat Jul 25, 3:30 PM (Driver: Simona, 25+). Drop-off Fri Jul 31, 8:30 AM. Intermediate (Toyota Corolla or similar), automatic. $700.92, pay at pickup.

eventsAndTransport.where: Citizens Bank Park — Section 320, Row 2, seats 13–15 (Simona, Theo & Silke).
eventsAndTransport.detail: Pre-game: 4:30 PM early dinner at Pizzeria Beddia, 1313 N Lee St, Fishtown (party of 5) — reserve ahead. Uber from Fishtown to ballpark (~15–20 min).

lodging.name: Ithaca Marriott Downtown on the Commons
Courtyard New Haven at Yale (30 Whalley Ave)
Canopy by Hilton Philadelphia Center City (1180 Ludlow St) — 2 Queen Premium

weather.conditions: Warm; Rochester Sun ~78°, mostly sunny after Sat storms

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1850 and relocated to its neoclassical River Campus in 1930, Rochester was shaped by one transformative donor — George Eastman of Kodak — whose gifts built the Eastman School of Music, the medical school, and much of the campus you'll walk today. His statue presides over the Eastman Quadrangle. Two Rochester figures anchor the university's moral throughline: Susan B. Anthony, whose advocacy helped open the university to women in 1900, and Frederick Douglass, who published the North Star in Rochester — both are memorialized on campus. Motto: Meliora — 'ever better.'
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: The Rochester Curriculum has no general-education core: beyond a major, students build two self-chosen 'clusters' (a trio of related courses) in each of the two divisions outside their major. This means Theo could pair a Brain & Cognitive Sciences or neuroscience major (a national flagship, with direct ties to his UCSD work) with deliberate clusters in philosophy and political science — rather than checking distribution boxes. All four of his interests live in one degree. The 'Rochester School' (William Riker's rational-choice tradition) anchors the poli-sci side; the Institute of Optics was the nation's first. Undergraduate research is the norm. Two signature programs extend the runway: Take Five (a tuition-free extra semester/year outside the major) and the Kauffman Entrepreneurial Year (KEY). Rochester also awards open merit scholarships — relevant given the family's aid picture.

walkingTour.parking: Free weekend parking at the Library / Susan B. Anthony lots

stops.name: Wilson Commons
stops.notes: I.M. Pei glass-atrium student union — grab a campus map here. The social hub of River Campus.

stops.name: Frederick Douglass Commons
stops.notes: Main dining hub + Douglass statue. He published the North Star in Rochester — this is where students gather daily.

stops.name: Susan B. Anthony Halls
stops.notes: First-year residence complex. The namesake helped win coeducation in 1900. Good sense of the residential-community feel.

stops.name: Rush Rhees Library
stops.notes: 1930 domed landmark — step inside the rotunda and Great Hall. The intellectual core of campus; architecturally the most striking building.

stops.name: Eastman Quadrangle
stops.notes: Historic academic heart. George Eastman statue, Kodak colonnade, Dewey / Morey / Lattimore halls surrounding the open quad.

stops.name: Meliora Hall
stops.notes: Home of Brain & Cognitive Sciences and neuroscience — Theo's direct tie to his UCSD work. Research-forward; note the lab signage.

stops.name: Harkness Hall
stops.notes: Political Science & Philosophy — home of the 'Rochester School' of rational-choice theory (William Riker). Theo's poli-sci and philosophy base.

stops.name: Hutchison Hall
stops.notes: Biology & chemistry, beside the Hajim engineering quad and Wegmans Hall (data science). The STEM core — loop back to Wilson Commons from here.

collegeBriefings.history: Chartered in 1865 by Ezra Cornell and Andrew Dickson White, Cornell was radical for its era — nonsectarian and, early on, coeducational — and built on Ezra's credo: 'I would found an institution where any person can find instruction in any study.' That 'any person, any study' ethos still defines the place. It is the youngest Ivy and a hybrid — a private university that also houses New York's land-grant colleges — set on a dramatic hillside above Cayuga Lake and laced with gorges. The Arts Quad is the founding heart, where the Ezra Cornell and A.D. White statues face each other and the 173-foot McGraw Tower chimes daily over Uris Library.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: For Theo, the draw is breadth with depth. Applicants choose one undergraduate college up front — for his interests, the College of Arts & Sciences — which keeps requirements flexible and lets students combine fields (the College Scholar Program even lets a few design their own major). All four of his interests run deep here: Government (political science) in White Hall is nationally ranked, Philosophy sits in Goldwin Smith Hall, Psychology and Neurobiology & Behavior cover the neuroscience side (his UCSD through-line), and Cornell Law School's Gothic quad at Myron Taylor Hall is a visible draw for the law interest. One honest caveat given your aid picture: like the other Ivies, Cornell awards need-based aid only — no merit scholarships — so it belongs on the list for fit, research, and prestige rather than as a financial play.

walkingTour.duration: ~75 min (guided tour covers most of this)
walkingTour.startNote: Tour departs from the Tang Welcome Center at Noyes Lodge — stops below follow the standard tour route
walkingTour.parking: Forest Home Garage (reserved ParkMobile ePermit, valid all day Jul 20)

stops.name: Ho Plaza & Willard Straight Hall
stops.notes: The social crossroads and student union; where the guided tours end. Read the campus pulse.

stops.name: Uris Library & McGraw Tower
stops.notes: The 173-ft clocktower (daily chimes concerts) over Uris Library's A.D. White Reading Room. The icon.

stops.name: Arts Quad
stops.notes: The founding core — Ezra Cornell & A.D. White statues face off. 'Any person, any study.'

stops.name: White Hall
stops.notes: Department of Government (political science) — nationally ranked. Theo's poli-sci base.

stops.name: Goldwin Smith Hall
stops.notes: Humanities: Philosophy, Classics, English (Klarman Hall atrium attached). Theo's philosophy base.

stops.name: Uris Hall
stops.notes: Psychology, incl. behavioral / cognitive neuroscience — Theo's UCSD neuro tie.

stops.name: Olin & Kroch Libraries
stops.notes: The main research libraries — the study engine. Loop back toward Ho Plaza.

stops.name: Myron Taylor Hall
stops.notes: Cornell Law School's Gothic quad — the law-interest anchor, at the south edge of central campus.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1636, Harvard is the oldest university in the United States — older than the nation itself. Its heart is Harvard Yard, the elm-shaded quad you'll walk: the John Harvard statue outside University Hall (the 'statue of three lies'), the monumental Widener Library facing Memorial Church across Tercentenary Theatre, and the red brick and ivy that set the template for the American campus. Harvard Square spills out just beyond Johnston Gate.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: For Theo, Harvard College's strength is its range. Concentrations reach all four of his interests: Philosophy lives in Emerson Hall (right on the Yard), Government (political science) is a national powerhouse, the interfaculty Mind/Brain/Behavior initiative and Psychology in William James Hall anchor the neuroscience side, and Harvard Law School's Langdell quad sits a short walk northwest. Students braid a path across the College plus its libraries and museums. The honest caveat, as with the other Ivies: Harvard offers need-based aid only — no merit scholarships — so it belongs on the list for fit and prestige rather than as a financial play.

walkingTour.startNote: Enter at Johnston Gate after the 11 AM info session at Radcliffe. Follow stops 1→8.
walkingTour.parking: Street parking not advised; use area garages (no campus lot for visitors)

stops.name: Johnston Gate
stops.notes: The ceremonial main gate into Harvard Yard — step through into the Old Yard.

stops.name: University Hall & John Harvard Statue
stops.notes: The 'statue of three lies'; rub the shoe. Granite University Hall behind it.

stops.name: Memorial Church
stops.notes: Faces Widener across Tercentenary Theatre — the Yard's ceremonial core.

stops.name: Widener Library
stops.notes: The monumental centerpiece — one of the world's largest university libraries.

stops.name: Emerson Hall
stops.notes: Home of the Philosophy department — Theo's philosophy base, right on the Yard.

stops.name: Sever Hall
stops.notes: H.H. Richardson's Romanesque classroom landmark; try the whispering arch.

stops.name: Memorial Hall
stops.notes: Soaring High-Victorian Gothic — Sanders Theatre + Annenberg first-year dining hall.

stops.name: Science Center Plaza
stops.notes: Tanner Fountain + science gateway; William James Hall (Psych/neuro) and the Law School lie just beyond.

collegeBriefings.history: Chartered in 1852 by Universalists on a breezy hill straddling Medford and Somerville, Tufts has always been small, independent, and a little quirky — its mascot is Jumbo, P.T. Barnum's legendary circus elephant, whose namesake Barnum Hall anchors the campus. From the President's Lawn the Boston skyline unfolds; Ballou Hall (1852) and the Romanesque Goddard Chapel crown the hilltop Academic Quad you'll tour.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: Tufts punches above its size for Theo's interests. It's nationally known for International Relations and Political Science (Packard Hall), Philosophy sits in Miner Hall, and a strong undergraduate Neuroscience program plus Psychology in Paige Hall cover the brain side — the through-line from his UCSD work. There's no law school, but the poli-sci / IR pipeline and pre-law advising feed law careers directly. Like the Ivies, though, Tufts awards need-based aid only — no merit scholarships — so weigh it as a fit choice against the merit schools.

walkingTour.startNote: Park in Dowling Hall Garage (419 Boston Ave). Tour check-in at Eaton Hall; follow stops 1→8 around the Academic Quad.
walkingTour.parking: FREE for registered visitors — Dowling Hall Garage, 419 Boston Ave. Register vehicle online day-of.

stops.name: Eaton Hall
stops.notes: Tour check-in (5 The Green); admissions. Arrive ~20 min early.

stops.name: The Green (Academic Quad)
stops.notes: The hilltop heart, framed by the founding buildings.

stops.name: Goddard Chapel
stops.notes: 1882 Romanesque chapel with a landmark tower.

stops.name: Ballou Hall
stops.notes: The 1852 original building — administration and the campus's founding core.

stops.name: Barnum Hall & Jumbo
stops.notes: Named for P.T. Barnum; the Jumbo statue — rub the trunk for luck.

stops.name: Packard Hall
stops.notes: Political Science + the renowned International Relations home — Theo's poli-sci base.

stops.name: Tisch Library
stops.notes: The main library; head up for the Boston skyline view.

stops.name: Miner & Paige Halls
stops.notes: Philosophy (Miner) and Psychology / neuroscience (Paige) — Theo's brain-and-philosophy corner.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1861 and rooted on the Charles since 1916, MIT built its identity around 'Mens et Manus' — mind and hand. The campus reads as one connected machine: the Great Dome over Killian Court, the half-mile Infinite Corridor slicing through the Maclaurin Buildings, and Frank Gehry's tumbling Stata Center. It's intense, collaborative, and unmistakably built for people who like to make things.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: MIT is STEM-first, but it hits more of Theo's interests than people expect. Brain & Cognitive Sciences (Building 46, with the McGovern and Picower neuroscience institutes) is among the best in the world — a direct extension of his UCSD work. Its Philosophy group (within Linguistics & Philosophy, housed in Stata) is a top analytic-philosophy department, and Political Science (Course 17) is strong and quantitative. There's no law school, but the poli-sci path feeds law. As with the Ivies, MIT is need-based only — no merit aid — so treat it as a reach-for-fit, not a merit play.

walkingTour.startNote: Tour meets at the MIT Welcome Center, 292 Main St (Kendall). Loop 1→8 runs west into the main campus.
walkingTour.parking: Walk from your hotel (~6 min) — no need to move the car

stops.name: MIT Welcome Center
stops.notes: Tour check-in, 292 Main St (Kendall) — a ~6-min walk from your hotel.

stops.name: Stata Center (Bldg 32)
stops.notes: Gehry's landmark — home of CS and Linguistics & Philosophy (Theo's philosophy).

stops.name: Building 46
stops.notes: Brain & Cognitive Sciences + McGovern & Picower neuroscience institutes — his UCSD through-line.

stops.name: Great Dome & Barker Library
stops.notes: The iconic dome over Barker Library — MIT's signature image.

stops.name: Killian Court
stops.notes: The great lawn framing the dome, opening to the Charles — commencement is held here.

stops.name: Lobby 7 / 77 Mass Ave
stops.notes: The grand entrance and start of the half-mile Infinite Corridor.

stops.name: MIT Chapel & Kresge
stops.notes: Eero Saarinen's cylindrical chapel and shell-roofed auditorium.

stops.name: Stratton Student Center
stops.notes: The student hub (W20) — dining, the Coop, and campus life.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1839, BU is a big, urban, energetic university strung for a mile and a half along Commonwealth Avenue, with the Green Line trolley running right down the median. Martin Luther King Jr. earned his PhD here; Marsh Chapel and its plaza are the symbolic heart, and the new stacked-glass Center for Computing & Data Sciences (the 'Jenga tower') is the modern landmark. It feels like part of the city, not walled off from it.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: For Theo, BU is a strong all-around fit — and, unlike most of this week's schools, it offers real merit aid (Trustee and Presidential Scholarships), which matters here. Neuroscience, Philosophy, and Political Science all live in the College of Arts & Sciences, its pre-law advising is deep, and BU even has its own School of Law on campus. Big-school scale, but with an honors college (Kilachand) and merit money on the table make it worth a serious look.

walkingTour.startNote: Park at Agganis Arena Garage (925 Comm Ave). Walk the Comm Ave spine 1→8 east and loop back — stops 6–8 are 'if you have time.'

stops.name: George Sherman Union
stops.notes: The student union — grab a quick lunch at the food court here.

stops.name: Mugar Memorial Library
stops.notes: The main undergraduate library on Comm Ave.

stops.name: BU Beach
stops.notes: The riverside green — BU's informal gathering lawn.

stops.name: Marsh Plaza & Chapel
stops.notes: The symbolic heart; MLK Jr. memorial. BU earned him his 1955 PhD.

stops.name: College of Arts & Sciences
stops.notes: Neuroscience, Philosophy, and Political Science all live here — Theo's core.

stops.name: Warren Towers
stops.notes: The landmark first-year residence on Comm Ave (garage closed for repairs).

stops.name: Computing & Data Sciences
stops.notes: The stacked-glass 'Jenga tower' — BU's boldest new building.

stops.name: Questrom School of Business
stops.notes: East-end anchor; the walk back points you toward BC.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1863 by the Jesuits and moved to its Chestnut Hill hilltop in 1913, Boston College is a Catholic liberal-arts university famous for its 'Collegiate Gothic' architecture — Gasson Hall's tower is the icon, and Bapst Library's Gargan Hall is one of the most beautiful reading rooms in the country. The campus is green, leafy, and traditional, six miles west of downtown at the end of the Green Line B.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: The Jesuit intellectual tradition makes BC unusually strong in Philosophy — a real draw for Theo — alongside a well-regarded Political Science department and a growing Neuroscience program; the core curriculum leans into big questions of ethics and meaning, and pre-law placement is strong. On money, be clear-eyed: BC is essentially need-based, with only its very selective Presidential Scholars Program offering merit — so weigh it as a fit and values choice more than a merit play.

walkingTour.startNote: Tour starts at Devlin Hall (2:30 PM). Park at Commonwealth Ave Garage (GPS '2004 Campanella Way'), rooftop → O'Neill Plaza → Devlin. Loop 1→8 around Middle Campus.
walkingTour.parking: Commonwealth Ave Garage — GPS '2004 Campanella Way,' floors 3–6, elevator to 7th-floor rooftop. If full → Beacon Street Garage.

stops.name: Devlin Hall
stops.notes: Undergraduate Admission + tour check-in — ~2 min from the garage rooftop.

stops.name: Gasson Hall
stops.notes: The Gothic tower and quad — BC's iconic heart.

stops.name: Bapst Library & Gargan Hall
stops.notes: A breathtaking Gothic reading room — don't skip the interior.

stops.name: St. Mary's Hall
stops.notes: The Jesuit community residence anchoring the quad.

stops.name: O'Neill Library
stops.notes: The main research library, above O'Neill Plaza.

stops.name: Stokes Hall
stops.notes: Humanities home — Philosophy, Political Science, History (Theo's core).

stops.name: Higgins Hall
stops.notes: The sciences building — Biology and the Neuroscience program.

stops.name: Fulton Hall & the Quad
stops.notes: The business school and the classic Middle Campus lawn view.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1764, Brown is the seventh-oldest college in the country, set on College Hill above downtown Providence. Its signature is the Open Curriculum: there are no distribution requirements — students design their own path and take everything for a grade or Satisfactory/No Credit. The College Green, University Hall (1770, used as a barracks in the Revolution), and the Van Wickle Gates (opened inward at Convocation, outward at Commencement) anchor a relaxed, intellectually independent culture.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: For Theo, the Open Curriculum is the headline: with four interests, he could genuinely braid neuroscience, philosophy, political science, and pre-law without fighting a core. Brown is strong across all of them — the Carney Institute for Brain Science and Cognitive Sciences anchor neuro, and the outcomes are striking (about 83% of applying seniors get into law school). The honest caveat is familiar: as an Ivy, Brown is need-based only — no merit scholarships — so it's a fit-and-prestige choice rather than a merit play.

walkingTour.duration: ~75 min (guided tour covers most of this)
walkingTour.startNote: Tour meets at the Admission Welcome Center, 21 Prospect St. Walk ~8 min from Power Street Garage (111 Power St).
walkingTour.parking: Power Street Garage, 111 Power St (~$18/day flat rate)

stops.name: Admission Welcome Center
stops.notes: Tour check-in, 21 Prospect St — ~10-min uphill walk from the Power Street Garage.

stops.name: John Hay Library
stops.notes: The humanities research library — Poe collection, Lincoln papers, and the H.P. Lovecraft manuscripts. Beautiful reading rooms.

stops.name: Van Wickle Gates
stops.notes: Brown's ceremonial entrance on Prospect St — open only for Convocation and Commencement. The symbolic threshold of a Brown education.

stops.name: University Hall
stops.notes: The 1770 founding building — used as a barracks in the Revolutionary War. The administrative core of Brown.

stops.name: Sayles Hall
stops.notes: Concerts, lectures, and campus events — the public-facing heart of campus life on the Green.

stops.name: Stephen Robert '62 Campus Center
stops.notes: Student life hub — the everyday pulse of the Open-Curriculum culture.

stops.name: Metcalf Research Building
stops.notes: Cognitive & Psychological Sciences + Carney brain-science ties — Theo's neuro base.

stops.name: Sciences Library (SciLi)
stops.notes: The brutalist tower — the sciences study engine, with city views up top.

collegeBriefings.history: Founded in 1701, Yale is the third-oldest university in the country and the great cathedral of Collegiate Gothic — Harkness Tower, the nave-like Sterling Memorial Library, and Beinecke's translucent marble walls that glow from within. Undergraduates belong to one of 14 residential colleges (a model Harvard later copied), which become their home, dining hall, and community for four years. Old Campus, with 1750s Connecticut Hall and the Nathan Hale statue, is where first-years live.
collegeBriefings.programUniqueness: Yale may be the single best fit on this trip for Theo's mix. Political Science is a national powerhouse, Philosophy is top-ranked, the Wu Tsai Institute drives a strong Neuroscience track — and Yale Law School is the #1 law school in the country, an unmatched backdrop for a pre-law student even as an undergrad. The one catch is money: as an Ivy, Yale is need-based only, with no merit scholarships, so it belongs on the list for fit and reach rather than as a financial play.

walkingTour.startNote: Tour departs from SSS Hall, 1 Prospect St (Grove St side). Park at Grove Street Garage, 65 Grove St (~3-min walk).
walkingTour.parking: Grove Street Garage, 65 Grove St (~3-min walk to SSS Hall)

stops.name: SSS Hall (Tour Start)
stops.notes: Sheffield-Sterling-Strathcona Hall, 1 Prospect St — Grove St side entrance. Tour check-in; QR code ready.

stops.name: Beinecke Rare Book Library
stops.notes: The landmark 1963 library — walls of translucent Vermont marble filter light onto a Gutenberg Bible. The most architecturally singular building in New Haven.

stops.name: Sterling Law Building
stops.notes: Yale Law School's Gothic quad — ranked #1 in the country. The law-interest anchor; the courtyard is open to visitors.

stops.name: Sterling Memorial Library
stops.notes: Yale's cathedral-scale main library — Gothic nave with stained glass, built in 1930. One of the great research libraries in the world.

stops.name: Cross Campus
stops.notes: The large open lawn at Yale's center — the site of Commencement and daily student life. Flanked by library, law, and residential colleges.

stops.name: Harkness Tower & Branford College
stops.notes: The 216-ft Collegiate Gothic tower that defines the New Haven skyline. Branford College's courtyard is one of Yale's most beautiful residential spaces.

stops.name: Old Campus & Connecticut Hall
stops.notes: The 1750 Connecticut Hall is Yale's oldest surviving building — Nathan Hale lived here. Old Campus is where first-years live.

stops.name: Jonathan Edwards College
stops.notes: Yale's oldest residential college — the Gothic courtyard to end on. 68 High St, ~10-min walk from SSS Hall.
